title: Numpy weekmask should have a valid value
description: Ensure numpy weekmask has a valid value to prevent unexpected behavior.
message: Numpy weekmask should have a valid value.
recommendation: Use a valid value for the numpy weekmask.

noncompliant.code:
import numpy as np
mask = np.weekmask([0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6])
noncompliant.description: Incorrect weekmask usage with non-week-like sequence.

compliant.code:
import numpy as np
mask = np.weekmask([datetime.datetime(2022, 1, 1), datetime.datetime(2022, 1, 2), datetime.datetime(2022, 1, 3)])
compliant.description: Correct weekmask usage with datetime objects.

why_is_this_an_issue: Using an invalid weekmask can lead to unexpected behavior and potential data errors.
how_to_fix: Ensure weekmask is used with a valid sequence of datetime objects or integers representing days of the week.

logic.new_check_type_doc: Custom function to check if weekmask has a valid value.

function_code:
def is_valid_weekmask(node):
    import ast
    if isinstance(node, ast.Call) and node.func.id == 'weekmask' and
       (not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.List) or not all(isinstance(x, (ast.Num, ast.Constant)) for x in node.args[0].elts) and
       not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.Tuple) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.GeneratorExp) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.ListComp) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.IfExp) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.AnnAssign) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.Assign) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.For) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.While) and not isinstance(node.args[0], ast.With)):
        return True
    return False

metadata.title: Numpy weekmask should have a valid value
